action_set_definitions.name: get_environment_info
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the initial database schema, active indexes, and the target SQL query statement to identify the table and column names.

example_output.database_schema: CREATE TABLE events (id serial, data jsonb);

action_set_definitions.name: get_storage_metrics
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves table-level and system-level storage statistics, including total heap size and individual table sizes to calculate overhead constraints.

table_name.description: The name of the table to check.

action_set_definitions.name: inspect_json_column
action_set_definitions.description: Analyzes a JSONB/JSON column to identify specific keys, their cardinality (uniqueness), data types, and whether they are stored in TOAST (out-of-line).

action_set_definitions.name: apply_optimization
action_set_definitions.description: Executes a DDL command to optimize the database, such as creating a B-Tree index or adding a generated column.

ddl_command.description: The SQL DDL string (e.g., CREATE INDEX...).

action_set_definitions.name: verify_execution_plan
action_set_definitions.description: Runs EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) on the query to verify the execution path and TOAST block interactions.

state_description.database_schema: The DDL structure of tables, including column names and data types.
state_description.active_indexes: A list of currently existing indexes in the database environment.
state_description.query_statement: The current SQL query string that is being optimized.
state_description.table_statistics: Metadata regarding table sizes, row counts, and column cardinality used for cost simulation.
